key_insight: Symmetry in electromagnetic devices can be turned into exact transformation rules and equivariant neural networks, making scattering prediction far more data-efficient and physically consistent.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate models for photonic and electromagnetic design, reducing dependence on expensive full-wave simulations during optimization.
method: Derive symmetry-to-scattering transformation rules from Maxwell’s equations.
Use symmetry-based data augmentation for standard neural networks.
Build exactly equivariant models for both discrete and continuous symmetry groups.
evidence_signal: On photonic-crystal slabs and free-form diffraction gratings, symmetry-informed models improved data efficiency by about an order of magnitude versus standard architectures.
watch_item: Performance depends on correctly identifying and encoding the relevant device symmetries; mismatches or broken symmetries can limit the benefit.
paper_title: Symmetry-Informed Deep Learning for Electromagnetic Scattering
